72#YZ|## CONVENTIONS
73#JR|
74#YV|- Runtime and scripts are pnpm + Node-first (`packageManager: pnpm@10.x`); prefer `pnpm run <script>` and `node --import tsx` for TypeScript entrypoints.
75#HP|- Canonical quality flow is `check` (non-mutating) and `lint` (mutating via `ultracite fix`).
76#QJ|- Tests are colocated in package `src/**` trees as `*.test.ts` and executed with `vitest` via package scripts.
77#PQ|- `tsconfig.json` enforces `strict` in each package; do not treat `dist/` as source-of-truth.
78#JY|- Legacy code should always be fully deprecated; aggressive updates without backward-compatibility guarantees are acceptable.
79#PB|- Package build order: `harness` then `tui` and `headless` (both depend on harness), then `cea` (depends on all three).
80#KK|
81#VK|## ANTI-PATTERNS (THIS PROJECT)
82#MP|
83#BM|- Editing generated outputs (`dist/`, `packages/*/dist/`) as if they were source code.
84#KQ|- Using shell commands (`cat`, `sed`, `rm`, `find`, `grep`) for file operations that dedicated tools already cover.
85#ZK|- Stopping at planning/todo updates without executing the concrete actions.
86#TR|- For benchmark work: changing event types without updating trajectory conversion rules in `packages/cea/benchmark/harbor_agent.py`.
87#QM|- Importing from `@ai-sdk-tool/cea` inside `harness`, `tui`, or `headless` — dependency direction is one-way.
88#YJ|
89#YP|## UNIQUE STYLES
90#ZY|
91#SB|- File edits in CEA favor hashline-aware operations (`LINE#HASH` + `expected_file_hash`) for stale-safe modifications.
92#TK|- Manual tool-loop continuation is intentionally constrained to finish reasons `tool-calls` and `unknown`.
93#KH|- Headless mode emits structured JSONL event types (`user`, `tool_call`, `tool_result`, `assistant`, `error`) consumed by benchmark tooling.
94#XY|- `SkillsEngine` discovers skills from up to five directories: bundled, global skills, global commands, project skills, project commands.
95#XK|

100#YQ|pnpm install

107#YN|# CEA-specific (from packages/cea or via workspace scripts)
